q:
believe
 Alan says he's 90 years old, but nobody ___ him.
a: Alan says he's 90 years old, but nobody believes him.

q:
not / remember
 She told me her name, but I ___ it now.
a: She told me her name, but I don't remember it now.

q:
use
 Don't put the dictionary away. I ___ it.
a: Don't put the dictionary away. I'm using it.

q:
need
 Don't put the dictionary away. I ___ it.
a: Don't put the dictionary away. I need it.

q:
consist
 Air ___ mainly of nitrogen and oxygen.
a: Air consists mainly of nitrogen and oxygen.

q:
want
 Who is that man? What ___ he ___?
a: Who is that man? What does he want?

q:
look
 Who is that man? Why ___ he ___ at us?
a: Who is that man? Why is he looking at us?

q:
recognise
 Who is that man? ___ you ___ him?
a: Who is that man? Do you recognise him?

q:
think
 I ___ of selling my car. Would you be interested in buying it?
a: I'm thinking of selling my car. Would you be interested in buying it?

q:
think
 I can't make up my mind. What ___ you ___ I should do?
a: I can't make up my mind. What do you think I should do?

q:
seem
 Gary wasn't well earlier, but he ___ OK now.
a: Gary wasn't well earlier, but he seems OK now.

q:
think
 Are you OK? You look worried. What ___ you ___ about?
a: Are you OK? You look worried. What are you thinking about?

q:
belong
 Who ___ this umbrella ___ to?
a: Who does this umbrella belong to?

q:
smell
 This food ___ good.
a: This food smells good.

q:
sit
 Excuse me. ___ anybody ___ there?
a: Excuse me. Is anybody sitting there?

q:
not / fit
 These gloves ___ me. They're too small.
a: These gloves don't fit me. They're too small.

q:
believe
 It's not true. I ___ it.
a: It's not true. I don't believe it.

q:
taste
 I've never eaten that fruit. What ___ it ___ like?
a: I've never eaten that fruit. What does it taste like?

q:
see
 Look over there. What ___ you ___?
a: Look over there. What do you see?

q:
be
 You'll like Sophie when you meet her. She ___ very nice.
a: You'll like Sophie when you meet her. She is very nice.

q:
be
 Sarah ___ very nice to me at the moment. I wonder why.
a: Sarah is being very nice to me at the moment. I wonder why.

q:
be
 They ___ very happy. They've just got married.
a: They are very happy. They've just got married.

q:
be
 You're normally very patient, so why ___ you ___ so unreasonable about waiting?
a: You're normally very patient, so why are you being so unreasonable about waiting?

q:
be
 Would you like something to eat? ___ you ___ hungry?
a: Would you like something to eat? Are you hungry?
